title: cracca obcordata
intro: Cracca obcordata, also known as the Kidney Vetch or Milkwort, is a flowering plant belonging to the pea family. Traditionally, Cracca obcordata has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant and diuretic effects. The health benefits of this plant include the relief of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its use in treating kidney stones and urinary tract infections. In herbal preparations, Cracca obcordata can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
